1.texto_original: Pero Satanás se levantó contra Israel, e incitó a David a que contase a Israel.
1.normal:
Devotional Study of 1 Crónicas 21:1

"Pero Satanás se levantó contra Israel, e incitó a David a que contase a Israel."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 1 Crónicas 21:1

Philological Analysis

"Pero Satanás se levantó contra Israel, e incitó a David a que contase a Israel."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Pero Satanás se levantó contra Israel, e incitó a David a que contase a Israel."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

12.texto_original: Escoge para ti, o tres años de hambre; o por tres meses ser derrotado delante de tus enemigos, y que la espada de tus adversarios te alcance; o por tres días la espada de Jehová y pestilencia en la tierra, y que el ángel de Jehová destruya en todo el término de Israel. Mira, pues, qué he de responder al que me ha enviado.

15.texto_original: Y envió Jehová el ángel a Jerusalén para destruirla; pero cuando él estaba destruyendo, miró Jehová, y se arrepintió de aquel mal, y dijo al ángel que destruía: ¡Basta ya! Detén tu mano. Y el ángel de Jehová estaba junto a la era de Ornán el jebuseo.
15.normal:
Devotional Study of 1 Crónicas 21:15

"Y envió Jehová el ángel a Jerusalén para destruirla; pero cuando él estaba destruyendo, miró Jehová, y se arrepintió de aquel mal, y dijo al ángel que destruía: ¡Basta ya! Detén tu mano. Y el ángel de Jehová estaba junto a la era de Ornán el jebuseo."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
15.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 1 Crónicas 21:15

Philological Analysis

"Y envió Jehová el ángel a Jerusalén para destruirla; pero cuando él estaba destruyendo, miró Jehová, y se arrepintió de aquel mal, y dijo al ángel que destruía: ¡Basta ya! Detén tu mano. Y el ángel de Jehová estaba junto a la era de Ornán el jebuseo."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.

Exegetical Analysis of 1 Crónicas 21:28

Philological Analysis

"En aquel tiempo cuando David vio que Jehová le había respondido en la era de Ornán jebuseo, sacrificó allí."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
28.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"En aquel tiempo cuando David vio que Jehová le había respondido en la era de Ornán jebuseo, sacrificó allí."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

29.texto_original: Y el tabernáculo de Jehová que Moisés había hecho en el desierto, y el altar del holocausto, [estaban] entonces en el alto de Gabaón:
29.normal:
Devotional Study of 1 Crónicas 21:29

"Y el tabernáculo de Jehová que Moisés había hecho en el desierto, y el altar del holocausto, [estaban] entonces en el alto de Gabaón:"

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.

30.texto_original: Pero David no pudo ir allá a consultar a Dios, porque estaba espantado a causa de la espada del ángel de Jehová.
30.normal:
Devotional Study of 1 Crónicas 21:30

"Pero David no pudo ir allá a consultar a Dios, porque estaba espantado a causa de la espada del ángel de Jehová."
